engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking a PLACE — which cafe is near them, its address, phone and hours — and deciding whether to visit or order. Browsing locations/hours IS the decision, so place wins over quick-yes despite the redirect CTAs.

goal.one_action: Get the visitor to find a nearby cafe (and its hours) or tap 'Order Online' to order coffee

structure.archetype_note: Brand/place site for a multi-location coffee chain; home teases locations + an external Order Online link, contact page carries the address/phone/hours tables, shop redirects to external Square/Tweed stores.

mapped.observed_as: THE PATTERN Of Coffee and people

unmapped.observed: Cafe Locations grid + Dallas/Austin address-phone-hours tables
unmapped.why_no_fit: There is no canonical 'locations' section; a multi-branch listing with address/phone/hours is core to a place-of-business site but maps only loosely to miscellaneous

design_style.none_fits_because: No design hints captured (fonts/colors empty); warm/human inferred from a community-oriented coffee-shop brand, low certainty

confidence.why: fetch_quality is partial (rendered_pages empty, submit-button and HQ contact details not captured); core structure, engine and goal are clear from verbatim sections but design/proof signals are thin

gap_findings: No canonical section expresses a multi-location address/phone/hours listing — a place-business staple; currently forced into miscellaneous. map-hours capability exists but there is no matching sectionList id.

Site has several parallel redirect CTAs (Order Online → Linktree, Shop Products → Square, Shop Coffee → Tweed) — the single-'one_action' model does not cleanly capture a place site that fans out to multiple external ordering/commerce destinations.
